skill.name: html-structure-validate
skill.description: Validate generated HTML5 pages for required structure, metadata, tag closure, content sections, lists, links, images, and tables before a pipeline continues.
skill.summary: Run a deterministic HTML5 structure quality gate for generated page files.
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review shows they are Markdown examples, HTML tags, relative paths, and normal validation instructions. The real residual risk is low: the skill asks an agent to run a local Python validator and read or write local project files, with no evidence of network use, credential access, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Command Execution
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run python3 validate_html.py for structure validation. This is legitimate for an HTML validation skill, but users should confirm the local validator path and input file before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution instruction is explicit and semantically tied to the validation purpose. Risk is limited because it targets a local validator and no dynamic shell composition or network transfer is shown.

low_findings.title: Local File Read and Report Write Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to read generated HTML files and save validation reports under local output directories. This is expected behavior, but it requires normal filesystem access controls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write behavior is directly documented. The paths are local project artifacts, and I found no evidence of sensitive file targeting or exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Patterns Dismissed as False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, path traversal, and Ruby backtick findings are caused by Markdown formatting, HTML examples, and relative asset paths. No executable weak cryptography or reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited sections are documentation and examples, not bundled implementation code. Confidence is high enough to dismiss the static alerts, while retaining low risk for the actual local command workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Invoked Local Validator Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell examples that run python3 validate_html.py against generated HTML files. This pattern is safe when the validator is trusted and paths are constrained to the project workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is real and intentionally documented. I found no shell interpolation, remote download, or suspicious destination in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Generated HTML can contain missing tags, weak document structure, or incomplete metadata. This skill checks page structure and produces a validation report before later pipeline steps run.
actual_capabilities: Checks for HTML5 doctype, html, head, body, title, charset, stylesheet link, and page container elements.
Validates tag closure, nesting, headings, lists, links, images, tables, and content presence.
Produces a structured validation report with pass, fail, warning, and summary information.
Defines blocking failure criteria that stop a generation pipeline when structural errors are found.
Documents how to run an existing local validate_html.py tool in structure validation mode.
limitations: Requires a local validate_html.py tool; the skill does not include the validator implementation.
Focuses on structural HTML checks, not visual rendering quality or accessibility conformance.
Uses project-specific paths and page naming conventions that may need adaptation.
Warnings and strict-mode behavior depend on the external validator implementation.

use_cases.title: Block Broken Generated Pages
use_cases.description: Stop downstream processing when generated HTML is missing required structure or contains malformed tags.

use_cases.title: Standardize Page Quality Reports
use_cases.description: Create consistent validation reports for each page artifact before chapter consolidation.

use_cases.title: Review Structural Failures
use_cases.description: Identify missing metadata, container elements, headings, lists, image attributes, and link attributes in generated pages.

prompt_templates.title: Validate One Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a single generated page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the html-structure-validate skill to validate this generated HTML page: [path]. Save the structure report in the page artifact directory.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Validation Failure
prompt_templates.scenario: Understand why a page failed the quality gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the html-structure-validate skill to review the validation report for [page]. Explain each blocking error and list the smallest fixes needed.

prompt_templates.title: Run Strict Structure Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the html-structure-validate skill in strict mode for [page]. Include warnings as failures and summarize what must change before publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Chapter Page Artifacts
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced batch review across a chapter
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the html-structure-validate skill to validate every generated page artifact in [chapter directory]. Summarize pass, fail, and warning trends across the chapter.

output_examples.input: A generated chapter page with correct document structure and one missing viewport warning.
output: One non-blocking viewport warning was reported.

output_examples.input: A generated page missing a title tag and closing body tag.
output: The pipeline should stop before consolidation.
Fix the missing title and unmatched body structure, then re-run validation.

output_examples.input: A chapter directory with multiple page artifacts ready for review.
output: Pages with malformed lists or missing stylesheet links need repair.
The summary separates blocking errors from warnings.

best_practices: Validate each generated HTML page before consolidation or visual review.
Keep input and output paths inside the intended project artifact directories.
Review blocking errors first, then decide whether warnings should fail the build.
anti_patterns: Do not run the validator on untrusted paths outside the project workspace.
Do not ignore a failed structure report and continue the publishing pipeline.
Do not treat this skill as a full accessibility, security, or browser rendering audit.

faq.question: Does this skill include an HTML validator?
faq.answer: No. It documents how to use an existing local validate_html.py tool in structure validation mode.

faq.question: What happens when validation fails?
faq.answer: The documented workflow stops the pipeline and asks the user to review and fix the reported errors.

faq.question: Can it validate any HTML file?
faq.answer: It can be adapted, but the instructions assume project-specific page artifact paths and naming conventions.

faq.question: Does it check visual layout?
faq.answer: No. It checks document structure, required elements, tag closure, and related structural rules.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence of network requests was found in the reviewed skill file.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low instead of safe?
faq.answer: The skill instructs an agent to run a local command and access local files, so normal workspace trust controls still matter.
